master_row.title: Emergent Rotation Principle
master_row.canonical_statement: Rotation is generated continuously through electromagnetic torques rather than being an initial condition.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an integral expression relating V, r, J, x, B.
master_row.dictionary: The total angular momentum (L) of a system in a Trawinistic manifold is not a primordial conserved quantity but emerges via (L = _V (r),d^3x) with (J) driven by local electromagnetic fields according to (dLdt=_V (J B),d^3x).
master_row.encyclopedia: Rotation is treated as an emergent accumulated effect of local electromagnetic current and field interactions rather than as a primordial conserved input. Interpretive role: This principle opens the rotation-and-magnetism subchain of the registry. It supports the Elsasser attractor, equipartition, and the later universal scaling laws by treating rotation as an effect of structured current flow.
